heroHeadline: Thomson Reuters Employee Fired for Raising ICE Data Concerns; FY2025 Removal Total Hits 442,637
heroSubtitle: NPR investigation finds Billie Little, a nearly two-decade Thomson Reuters employee, was fired after raising concerns that ICE may be misusing the company's CLEAR database — which holds license plate, SSN, and address data — during the Minneapolis enforcement surge that killed two people; company holds a $5M ICE contract for 'license plate reader data to enhance investigations for potential arrest'; FY2025 total removals of 442,637 (formal + voluntary returns) released under Trump 2.0; ICE sets official annual target of 1 million deportations; UCLA study finds noncriminal Latino detentions up sixfold under Trump; ICE custody deaths remain at 29 in FY2026, a 22-year record
footerNote: Data sourced from DHS, ICE official records, ACLU, HRW, DHS OIG, TRAC Reports, Detention Watch Network, court records, and Congressional records
